codeReview.summary: The config example and watcher synthesizer changes cover the commented placeholder example, config/default-base handling, and blank-key skipping for the watcher synthesizer path. However, file-backed auth loading through sdk/auth and the internal store backends still creates active Z.AI auth records with auth_kind=apikey and a default base_url when api_key/api-key is missing or blank, and the Z.AI executor does not reject an empty api key before dispatch. This leaves a usable/routable auth record that can reach execution without Authorization, so VAL-ZAI-004 is not satisfied.

issues.description: applyZAIFileAPIKeyAttributes sets auth_kind=apikey and default base_url for provider zai but does not skip or disable the auth when api_key/api-key is absent or blank. git/object/postgres store readers call this helper and then append/return the active auth, so blank/missing Z.AI auth-file entries can remain routable despite VAL-ZAI-004 requiring store paths to skip or mark them unusable before execution.

The SDK file token store has a duplicate applyZAIFileAPIKeyAttributes implementation with the same behavior: missing or blank api_key/api-key leaves an active zai auth with auth_kind=apikey and default base_url but no api_key attribute. This path was not hardened by commit 475d3d54 and fails the auth-file hardening requirement for sdk/auth.

Execution only adds Authorization when apiKey is non-empty and otherwise continues to POST to the upstream base URL; the streaming path has the same pattern at line 203. If an active auth from the file/git/object/postgres stores lacks api_key, it is not rejected before execution, contrary to the requirement that blank/missing Z.AI API keys cannot synthesize usable auth records or must be marked unusable before execution.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Z.AI file-auth normalization is duplicated in sdk/auth/filestore.go and internal/store/zai_auth.go, while watcher synthesis has a separate implementation. The mission guidance states file/git/object/postgres store paths are evidence for VAL-ZAI-004, but AGENTS.md does not call out that Z.AI auth-file hardening must be kept consistent across all three places.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: internal/watcher/synthesizer/file.go:162-191 was hardened to return nil on blank keys; internal/store/zai_auth.go:18-39 and sdk/auth/filestore.go:284-305 still set auth_kind/base_url without skipping or disabling missing-key auths.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The reviewed worker session was recorded as a manual orchestrator repair because the worker repeatedly failed EndFeatureRun by omitting the required top-level commitId, despite the go-provider-worker skill explicitly warning that successful handoff must include commitId at top level.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false with deviation step EndFeatureRun; .factory/skills/go-provider-worker/SKILL.md step 6 says EndFeatureRun must include the feature commit hash in the top-level commitId field.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The transcript skeleton for the assigned completed worker session e42ed717-549d-420d-970b-edd488e9a789 is absent from worker-transcripts.jsonl, so the review could not verify that exact session's transcript skeleton. The available transcript entries for this feature show earlier worker sessions and repeated reads of config.example.yaml.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: jq listing of worker-transcripts.jsonl contained fa6ce40d, 8617021c, e4d365ae, ed113f6c, and ee27c883 only; no e42ed717 entry was present. The available feature transcript shows many repeated Read calls over config.example.yaml.

summary: Fail: commits 530ecf9d and 475d3d54 cover the config example and watcher synthesizer path, but they do not harden SDK/internal store-backed Z.AI auth-file records or executor behavior against missing API keys, leaving VAL-ZAI-004 unmet.
